codeReview.summary: The fix commit addresses both prior scrutiny findings: handleNodeCompletion now rejects completion/failure unless the node is currently running, and create() now registers schedulers so activeScheduler(for:) aligns with documented behavior.

issues.description: The scheduler tests cover in-order completion flows but do not add a regression case for the previously failed scenario (completion/failure reported for non-running nodes) or for create()/activeScheduler registry behavior, so these fixes are not directly test-locked.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Skill procedure compliance appears loosely enforced for fix-only tasks: the worker marked followedProcedure=true even though the prescribed command set and test-first step were not followed exactly.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/skills/runtime-orchestration-worker/SKILL.md:18-25 requires "Add failing tests first" and focused commands including BurnBarRunServiceTests + BurnBarProviderRouterTests; handoff 2026-04-20T11-24-47-020Z...c7af0727...json:14-33 lists only VAL_EXEC_009, VAL_EXEC_013, BurnBarMissionControlServiceTests, and full package tests; same handoff line 45 sets followedProcedure=true.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Mission guidance says validators should come from services.yaml, but the feature-level verification commands used in practice are not represented there.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: /Users/albertonunez/.factory/missions/d1d1287f-f2a9-4a34-8ba9-fce37df13493/AGENTS.md:29 says to use commands from .factory/services.yaml as single source of truth; .factory/services.yaml has no VAL_EXEC_009/VAL_EXEC_013 entries; fix handoff commandsRun includes those filters.

summary: Pass: the re-run fix resolves the prior blocking running-state guard issue and the create()/activeScheduler registry drift; one non-blocking test-coverage gap remains for direct regression locking.
